description:
Model a real-world inter-building shading scenario for a solar PV feasibility study in SketchUp Make 2017 using the Skelion plugin.

1. Draw Building A (the solar installation site): Draw a 15m x 10m rectangle on the ground and push/pull it up to 4m tall.
2. Draw Building B (the shading obstruction): Starting 8m to the south of Building A's southern edge (along the green axis), draw a 20m x 12m rectangle and push/pull it up to 14m tall.
3. Select Building A's roof face (the shorter building) and use Skelion to insert solar panels in landscape orientation at a 0 degree tilt.
4. Configure shadows: Open the Shadows dialog (Window > Shadows), enable shadow display, set the date to December 21, and time to 12:00 PM.
5. Set the model's geographic location to Denver, Colorado, USA (39.74 N, 104.99 W) via Window > Model Info > Geo-location > Set Manual Location.
6. Save the model to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\shadow_analysis.skp'.
